_comment: REFERENCE / FALLBACK ONLY — you normally do NOT need to install this. As of DRY-12 the daemon injects these same hooks into every claude session via `claude --settings` (daemon/src/hooks.ts), so they work in any cwd with no per-repo setup. This file documents the wiring and is a manual fallback if you ever run claude outside Drydock. The daemon injects DRYDOCK_SESSION_ID + DRYDOCK_DAEMON_URL into the session env. PreToolUse pipes Claude Code's payload to the daemon and returns its decision (curl -m 590 stays under Claude Code's ~600s hook ceiling; widen the matcher e.g. "Bash|Write|Edit|WebFetch" to gate more tools). SessionStart (matcher "startup") asks the daemon for this session's ticket and injects its body as additionalContext (empty object = no-op for non-ticket sessions). NOTE: if you install this in a repo AND let the daemon inject via --settings, the hooks fire twice — pick one.
